items.title: Death toll rises in Pulau Pangkor migrant boat tragedy after rescuers recover four bodies, 10 still missing
items.summary: LUMUT, May 13 — Four bodies were recovered in the waters off Pulau Pangkor today during the second day of a search and rescue operation for victims of a capsized boat carrying illegal immigrants. Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) director, Maritime Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob, said the bodies were spotted by the Marine Police Force (PPM) at 2 pm. “Two PPM boats found the bodies floating some 13 nautical miles from Pulau Talang, near Segari.

items.title: Air Selangor contractor found lifeless in Taman Sentosa drain, Klang police say died likely from heart attack
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 13 — A man whose body was found in a drain at Taman Sentosa, Klang, yesterday afternoon is believed to have died of a heart attack. Klang Selatan district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said police received a report on the discovery of the local man’s body at 1.28 pm. “At 2.05 pm, an ambulance from Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital in Klang arrived at the scene and confirmed the man had died there.

items.title: KTM rider killed after crash with car in Kemaman
items.summary: District police chief Mohd Razi Rosli said the victim was from Kampung Chendor Baru, Kuantan, Pahang, and worked as a lorry driver. He said the victim was travelling from Bandar Chukai towards Chendor Baru at the time of the incident. "Upon reaching the traffic light junction there, the victim is said to have continued riding while the traffic light was flashing green.

items.title: Hajiji-led GRS names Masidi as deputy chairman after Pandikar quits, Armizan takes over as sec-gen
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 13 — Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah (PGRS) deputy president Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun, who is also Sabah Deputy Chief Minister II and state Finance Minister, has been appointed Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) deputy chairman. The four other GRS deputy chairmen are Parti Bersatu Sabah (PBS) president Datuk Seri Dr Joachim Gunsalam, Parti Cinta Sabah president Tan Sri Anifah Aman, Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah president Tan Sri Liew Yun Fah and Liberal Democratic Party president Datuk Chin Su Phin. PGRS president Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, who is also the Chief Minister, remains GRS chairman.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan closes Ulu Bendul Bendul Eco Park till May 24 over suspected leptospirosis scare
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 13 — The Ulu Bendul Forest Eco Park in Kuala Pilah has been temporarily closed to all activities until May 24 following reports of suspected leptospirosis, or rat urine disease, cases in the area. Negeri Sembilan Forestry Department director Mohd Yussainy Md Yusop said the closure would allow sanitation, thorough cleaning and water quality monitoring to be carried out to ensure visitors’ safety. Mohd Yussainy said all water activities at the eco park are prohibited throughout the closure period.

items.title: Geopolitical risks, oil price surge to keep KLCI range-bound, says HLIB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Heightened geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices, driven by the ongoing US-Iran impasse, are expected to keep the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI index in a range-bound consolidation phase after gaining 28.5 points month-to-date, according to Hong Leong Investment Bank (HLIB). The research house said market sentiment is likely to remain cautious and headline-driven after US President Donald Trump described the ceasefire as "on life support" amid renewed Gulf tensions and continued Israel-Lebanon border clashes. Investors will be closely watching corporate guidance as they weigh still-resilient fundamentals against rising geopolitical risks, elevated energy prices, and emerging growth headwinds," HLIB said in a note.

items.title: Jho Low seeks Trump pardon in bid to clear US criminal charges
items.summary: NEW YORK, May 13 — Malaysian financier Low Taek ‌Jho, widely known as Jho Low and now a ‌fugitive, has sought a pardon from US President Donald Trump, the Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported yesterday. Reuters could not immediately verify the report. — Reuters

items.title: Fugitive Malaysian financier Jho Low seeks Trump pardon, WSJ reports
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Malaysian financier Low Taek Jho, widely known as Jho Low and now a fugitive, has sought a pardon from US President Donald Trump, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday. A Justice Department website lists a pending request for a "Pardon after Completion of Sentence" under Taek Jho Low that was filed this year, the report said. A White House official said Low's request was not currently on the White House's radar, WSJ added.

items.title: Sexually assaulted teen trapped in Bukit Kemuning apartment freed after neighbour hears his cries for help; Shah Alam cops on hunt for suspect
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 13 — Police are tracking down a man believed to have confined and sexually assaulted a 14-year-old boy in an apartment in Bukit Kemuning, Section 32, here, Monday. Shah Alam police chief ACP Ramsay Embol said that investigations found that the suspect is believed to have deceived the victim by offering to buy the teenager a mobile phone internet package if he helped the man lift items at his house. “The victim was then tied up before being sexually assaulted by the suspect.

items.title: Better employee well-being leads to higher productivity, lower burnout
items.summary: The 29-year-old said she had endured emotional exhaustion and burnout after being overwhelmed by work demands, which gradually affected her mental well-being and personal life. Clarissa, who joined the company in 2022, said she initially enjoyed her job and was passionate about her work, but the situation changed after a Work-From-Home (WFH) policy was introduced in 2024. "Instead, it affects employees' mental health and makes them unhappy in their workplace.

items.title: Experts call for multi-dimensional income model in petrol subsidy review
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has been urged to adopt a multi-dimensional household income classification system to ensure the proposed petrol subsidy review for high-income earners is implemented transparently and accurately. Economist Emeritus Prof Dr Barjoyai Bardai said such an approach would provide a comprehensive assessment of financial capacity by accounting for assets, location, number of dependents, and spending patterns. He said integrating Padu data with Inland Revenue Board information would help the government identify recipients more accurately.

items.title: US processes US$35.5bil in refunds for tariffs imposed under IEEPA
items.summary: ISTANBUL: US authorities have processed about US$35.5 billion in refunds and interest for tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), which the Supreme Court later ruled did not authorise the tariffs. In a filing with the US Court of International Trade, US Customs and Border Protection said Tuesday that the first phase of its tariff refund system was opened for use on April 20, Anadolu Ajansi reported. The US Supreme Court ruled on Feb 20 that IEEPA, the law cited by Trump to impose the tariffs, did not authorise the president to levy such duties.

items.title: Trump says he does not consider economic impacts on Americans during Iran talks
items.summary: The US-Israeli war against Iran has cost Americans roughly US$38.1 billion in extra energy prices, according to figures compiled by the Iran War Energy Cost Tracker from Brown University's Watson School of International and Public Affairs. The majority of the extra costs – US$20.86 billion – have been paid for petroleum while diesel accounts for US$17.2 billion. The average US household has paid US$290.56 more per month across both gasoline and diesel.

items.title: Walmart lays off or relocates about 1,000 corporate workers
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Walmart would cut or relocate about 1,000 corporate workers as it looks to combine more of its global-technology and AI product teams, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the situation. Head of global AI acceleration Daniel Danker, and head of global technology Suresh Kumar have reviewed the retailer's internal structures and decided to streamline some teams to operate more efficiently, the WSJ report said, citing the leaders as saying in a memo sent to staff. Affected staff can apply for open roles within the company, the report said, citing the memo.

items.title: United Airlines to resume direct flights between US, Venezuela
items.summary: CARACAS, (Venezuela): US carrier United Airlines announced Tuesday that it plans to resume direct flights between the United States and Venezuela starting Aug. 11, marking its return to the route after nearly a decade, Xinhua reported. United Airlines' planned return follows American Airlines' resumption of direct commercial passenger flights to Venezuela on April 30, the first such US service after years of suspension. United Airlines suspended its Venezuela service in 2017 amid political tensions between Washington and Caracas.

items.title: Wall Street slips as inflation, Iran tensions weigh
items.summary: NEW YORK: The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq eased from record highs on Tuesday as hotter-than-expected inflation data and an increasingly tenuous US-Iran ceasefire prompted investors to take money off the table near the end of a robust first-quarter earnings season. While the PHLX Semiconductor index tumbled 4.8 per cent, the index has soared 62.4 per cent this year, benefiting from the fervor about artificial intelligence. Humana advanced 5.4 per cent following Bernstein's 36 per cent price target hike.

items.title: 'The silence is unbearable': Widow of FRU crash victim shares grief
items.summary: IPOH: A year after losing her husband in the Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) crash in Teluk Intan, Haswati Rosdin still struggles to accept that the man who once filled every corner of her life is gone. The silence left behind by Lance Corporal Damarrulan Abd Latif, who was then 33 — one of the nine FRU personnel killed in the crash — continues to haunt the 45-year-old widow, who said even the smallest routines now carry painful memories. Haswati said she eventually moved out from the police quarters in December because the surroundings constantly reminded her of her late husband.

items.title: 'No such thing as T20-only supply chain'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: There is a risk of "trickle-down" inflation arising from the possible removal of Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) subsidy for the T20 group, as fuel remains a key cost component in the national supply chain, an industry body said. "Even if the B40 and M40 groups continue to enjoy subsidised rates at the pump through Budi95, they are still likely to feel the impact through higher prices for daily goods and services as businesses adjust to these increased operating costs," he said. HARDEST HIT BY HIGHER FUEL COSTS Chin said the most affected segments would be last-mile industries including logistics, e-hailing, food delivery and physical distribution services.

items.title: 'They were shouting names': First responders recall FRU crash horror
items.summary: TELUK INTAN: The memory of the Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) convoy crash remains as heavy and vivid as ever for Fire and Rescue Department personnel who were among the first to arrive at the scene a year ago. Senior Fire Officer II Muhammad Fazli Shahril Che Ad, who was among the first responders, recalled arriving at a scene filled with confusion, urgency and grief. It was heartbreaking," he said.

items.title: Trump insists US doesn't need China's help in Iran
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump insisted Tuesday he does not need help from China on ending the war with Iran but said he would talk at length with Xi Jinping when they hold a summit this week. China is a top economic partner of Iran, buying much of its oil output, and also an important diplomatic backer. Amid reports that China continues to help arm Iran and purchase sanctioned oil, Trump said Xi has "been relatively good."

items.title: Second chance at life: FRU crash survivor returns to work
items.summary: IPOH: A year after surviving the Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) crash in Teluk Intan that claimed the lives of nine colleagues, Sergeant Mohd Parih Ali has finally returned to work. Parih said the journey to recovery had been long and painful — with three surgeries, nearly a year of medical leave and countless hospital appointments. "I underwent three surgeries and I'm still receiving treatment from orthopaedic and neurology specialists.

items.title: Tengku Adnan denies knowledge of KL retention pond land transfers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Tengku Adnan Tengku Mansor has denied any knowledge of flood retention pond land transfers to developers in the capital when he was the federal territories minister. He said land matters then were not under the ministry's purview. Tengku Adnan's comments came after Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said yesterday the capacity of flood retention ponds in Jinjang and Batu had shrunk to about 30 per cent of their original 33.5ha after 17 land lots were transferred to developers.

items.title: Kremlin says no 'specifics' on ending Ukraine war despite Putin's words
items.summary: MOSCOW: The Kremlin said Tuesday there was no concrete plan to end the Ukraine war, days after Russian leader Vladimir Putin suggested Europe's worst conflict since WWII could be winding down. Moscow and Kyiv resumed attacks overnight after the end of a three-day ceasefire proclaimed by US President Donald Trump, which each side accused the other of violating. Trump echoed this on Tuesday, saying the end of the war was "getting closer" and that he could potentially visit Russia this year.

items.title: Malaysians warn RON95 reform could hit all income groups
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians from all income backgrounds are increasingly worried that removing the RON95 fuel subsidy for the T20 income group could trigger spillover effects, including higher costs for lower- and middle-income households. He said rising fuel prices could eventually affect the wider public, especially as the government navigated economic uncertainty. He warned that poorly managed subsidy reforms could create broader economic ripple effects.

items.title: Not all T20 are wealthy, say earners as RON95 reform looms [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Many Malaysians associate the T20 label with financial comfort, luxury lifestyles and ample disposable income. Syakir said this benchmark did not reflect actual disposable income, particularly in urban centres. Syakir said about 80 per cent of his monthly income went to fixed commitments, including housing loans, transport costs, insurance and his children's education.

items.title: 'World faces year of severe fires'
items.summary: THE world could face a year of "particularly severe" wildfires — fuelled by climate change and a potentially strong El Nino weather phenomenon — after a record-breaking start to the year, researchers warned yesterday. And this is before El Nino has even formed. El Nino is the warm phase of a natural climate cycle in Pacific Ocean temperatures and trade winds, which influences global weather patterns and increases the likelihood of droughts, heavy rainfall and other climate extremes.

items.title: Liew clarifies RON95 remarks after quota cut confusion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians can rest assured that the RON95 subsidy quota will remain at 200 litres a month. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said his breakdown of the fuel use pattern at an event was misconstrued as him suggesting that the government's plan was to further reduce the quota to 150 litres. In March, following the spike in global crude oil prices, Putrajaya was forced to slash the 300 litres a month quota by 100 litres.

items.title: Goodbye long queues, hello couch: Malaysia’s government offices now fit in your phone
items.summary: Across the board, a wide range of agencies have shifted key services online, drastically reducing the need for physical queues and manual paperwork. Road Transport Department (JPJ) The JPJ has become a digital frontrunner. Road tax (LKM) renewals are now entirely digital, eliminating the need for the traditional physical sticker.

items.title: Toys in a (very big) box: How a simple steel container became a kids’ paradise in Petaling Jaya
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 13 — Inside a modest steel container at a low-cost flat in Mutiara Damansara, children of all ages gather around props, toy figurines, and children’s books — items many parents would consider luxuries secondary to essentials. Here at Apartment Desa Mutiara, a newly established toy library is more than just entertainment for children; it is also a space where children are not deprived of opportunities to play, learn, and imagine, similar to their more well-off peers. Accompanying Muhammad Qalish Mikhail was his mother, Linda Mat Desa, who looked on as he explored the toy library, taking time to engage with the activities and interact with other children in the space.

items.title: Trump 'Golden Dome' missile shield could cost US$1.2 trillion over 20 years
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump's "Golden Dome" missile defence shield could cost a staggering US$1.2 trillion over 20 years, the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office estimated on Tuesday. "Of the US$1.2 trillion amount, acquisition costs... would total just over US$1 trillion. Average annual costs for operations and support would meanwhile come in at US$8.3 billion, the report said.

items.title: 'Don't sabotage new chief commissioner'
items.summary: TAN SRI Azam Baki had a strong message for the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) on his final day heading the agency yesterday: "Don't sabotage the new chief commissioner". In his farewell address after six years at the helm, Azam, who turned 63 yesterday, urged the commission to fully back the new chief and senior management to ensure administrative continuity and preserve the agency's legacy. Former High Court judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman will take over as MACC chief today.

items.title: Big blow to KL flood prevention
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Flood retention ponds in Jinjang and Batu here have lost about 70 per cent of their original capacity after 17 plots around them were transferred to developers since 2015. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the original 33.5ha flood mitigation system had been significantly compromised. The Batu and Jinjang flood retention ponds form part of the larger Kuala Lumpur Flood Mitigation Scheme introduced in the early 2000s to prevent flash floods in the city.

items.title: Ekuinas makes RM7.1b in Bumiputera equity value
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ekuiti Nasional Bhd (Ekuinas) has generated Bumiputera equity value of RM7.1 billion and total shareholder value of RM8.5 billion since its inception, supported by an investment allocation of RM5.1 billion across 49 portfolio companies. This performance was backed by cumulative gains of RM5.7 billion, alongside a strengthened strategic portfolio — including the healthcare sector, which increased to 29 per cent of Ekuinas' active portfolio in 2025. Its proportion of Bumiputera employees in management positions within Ekuinas' portfolio companies rose to 36.9 per cent.

items.title: Saudi launched secret retaliatory attacks on Iran
items.summary: RIYADH: Saudi Arabia launched numerous, unpublicised strikes on Iran in retaliation for attacks carried out in the kingdom during the Middle East war, two Western officials briefed on the matter and two Iranian officials said. The attacks, launched by the Saudi Air Force, were assessed to have been carried out in late March, the two Western officials said. One said only that they were "tit-for-tat strikes in retaliation for when Saudi (Arabia) was hit."

items.title: Starmer clings to power as Labour rebellion deepens
items.summary: LONDON: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer battled to remain in his job Tuesday, as four junior ministers resigned their posts and dozens of lawmakers called for him to step down in the wake of heavy defeats in local and regional elections. But on Tuesday, more than 100 Labour members of parliament signed a statement backing their leader, highlighting the deep divisions within the beleaguered ruling party. "The Labour Party has a process for challenging a leader and that has not been triggered," Starmer told ministers.

items.title: French woman on ventilator after severe hantavirus infection
items.summary: PARIS: A French woman repatriated from a cruise ship struck by hantavirus was Tuesday in intensive care on a ventilator battling a severe case of the rare disease, a doctor said. The woman, one of five French passengers flown back from the MV Hondius and placed in isolation in Paris, started to feel very unwell on Sunday night and tested positive. "The patient now has the most severe form of cardiopulmonary presentation," Dr Xavier Lescure told a press conference at the health ministry.

items.title: Israel strikes Lebanon as authorities report 380 killed since truce
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel hammered south Lebanon with strikes on Tuesday ahead of talks between the two countries in Washington, as Beirut reported 380 people killed in Israeli attacks since an April 17 ceasefire took effect. Israel has intensified its attacks on south Lebanon, where it continues to trade fire with Hizbollah despite the ceasefire. Lebanon's Health Minister Rakan Nassereddine told a press conference on Tuesday that since the ceasefire, "380 people have been killed and 1,122 wounded."

items.title: Four Iranian Guards officers arrested in Kuwait over infiltration attempt
items.summary: KUWAIT CITY: Kuwait said on Tuesday that four people arrested earlier this month as they attempted to enter the country by sea confessed to belonging to Iran's Revolutionary Guards. The four men – two navy colonels, a captain and a lieutenant commander – admitted they had been tasked by Iran's ideological army with "infiltrating" Bubiyan Island, Kuwait's interior ministry said in a statement published by state news agency Kuna. The group attempted to enter on May 1 "aboard a fishing boat specially chartered to carry out hostile actions against Kuwait", the ministry said.

items.title: Works minister: Federal govt allocates RM2.4b for Sabah road projects under 13MP first rolling plan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 12 — The federal government is intensifying efforts to strengthen Sabah’s construction sector through greater investment, stricter project monitoring and enhanced industry capacity-building, said Works Minister Dato Sri Alexander Nanta Linggi. He said the ministry is committed to improving technical capabilities in Sabah to ensure smoother project delivery. We are committed, through the Ministry of Works, CIDB (Construction Industry Development Board) and the expertise from the Public Works Department (JKR), to assist Sabah.” Under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), the ministry has allocated RM2.4 billion for federal road projects in Sabah under the 2026 first rolling plan, which includes RM35.5 million for slope repair programmes, RM6.6 million for bridge projects, RM1.845 billion for road works and RM34.5 million for preliminary works.

items.title: Two sisters found drowned in Sungai Jeram Hulu in Terengganu after going missing while bathing
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 12 — Two sisters who went missing while bathing in Sungai Jeram Hulu near here were found drowned this evening. The bodies of Nur Afiqah Shahirah Mohd Shawalluddin, 15, and Nur Shakila Shahirah Azman, 12, from Kampung Jeram were recovered at about 6pm by the Water Rescue Team (PPDA) at a depth of four metres, some 20 metres from the scene of the incident. Terengganu Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations commander Baharum Hasim said a total of 14 personnel, including officers and PPDA members from the Kuala Terengganu and Kuala Nerus Fire and Rescue Stations, were involved in the search for the victims.

items.title: Azam Baki bids farewell to MACC, ‘deeply moved’ by officers’ and family’s support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Tan Sri Azam Baki clocked out of the Malaysian Anti‑Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters for the final time today as his tenure as chief commissioner officially ended. Azam urged all MACC personnel to give their full support and cooperation to the incoming leadership, saying the organisation must remain strong and relevant. “This organisation must remain relevant as envisioned by the earlier leadership.

items.title: Cops arrest four over fireworks at ‘Tangkap Azam Baki’ rally; case referred to AGC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The police have arrested four men over an incident last month where fireworks were set off during the “Tangkap Azam Baki” rally near the Sogo mall in Kuala Lumpur. Dang Wangi police chief ACP Sazalee Adam today said investigations are still ongoing, but also confirmed the arrest of four men as well as their release on police bail. Recommended reading: Rain dampens start of ‘Tangkap Azam Baki’ rally at Sogo, but surprise fireworks briefly cause chaos (VIDEO) At ‘Tangkap Azam Baki’ rally, protesters call for RCI, say fireworks were ‘sabotage’ Cops investigating fireworks during ‘Tangkap Azam Baki’ rally, unexploded ones found at otherwise ‘safe’ rally that drew 300 protesters

items.title: On final day as MACC chief, Azam Baki rules out politics and business in next chapter
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has officially ruled out a career in politics or the private sector, vowing instead to dedicate his retirement to lifelong public service. Speaking to reporters after his retirement ceremony today, Azam addressed long-standing speculation regarding his post-retirement plans, insisting he has no intention of transitioning into a businessman or a politician, despite being frequently asked about such prospects. Azam officially ended his duties today, marking the conclusion of a high-profile chapter in Malaysian law enforcement.

items.title: Anwar orders all ministries to retarget aid and protect vulnerable Malaysians from rising costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has issued a direct order to all ministries to realign their initiatives and assistance programmes, ensuring that government resources are precision-targeted to shield the poor and vulnerable from the escalating global supply crisis. Chairing the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting today, Anwar said that as the crisis puts increasing pressure on the prices of daily goods, it is imperative that no citizen is left behind. “Taking into account the impact of the crisis, which is increasingly putting pressure on the prices of daily goods, I have instructed every ministry to realign initiatives, efforts and assistance so that they reach the target groups and ensure no one is left behind, especially the poor and vulnerable groups,” he said in a statement online.

items.title: ‘No sabotage, please’: Azam Baki tells MACC to show absolute loyalty to successor
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has issued a stern reminder to his officers to extend their full, unwavering support to his successor, Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman. “There must be no sabotage of the new chief commissioner's leadership. Full cooperation must be given to the incoming leadership,” Azam said during his farewell address.

items.title: ‘Taken out of context’: Liew Chin Tong denies announcing new 150L Budi95 limit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong has clarified that he did not announce a reduced 150-litre Budi95 quota, saying that his remarks during a corporate event earlier today were taken out of context. The clarification follows media reports suggesting that the government was mulling specific fuel caps as part of its subsidy rationalisation strategy. Addressing the "media storm" caused by the reports, Liew explained that his comments were focused on data and long-term sustainability.

items.title: Thunderstorms forecast for KL, Selangor, and majority of states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Commuters and residents across the Klang Valley and several other states are advised to brace for heavy downpours and strong winds as the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued a widespread thunderstorm warning valid until 7:00 pm today. In the central region, the warning covers Kuala Lumpur and several districts in Selangor, specifically Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat. The weather warning extends significantly across the east coast and southern peninsula.

items.title: Founding was only half the story: Descendant says Yap Ah Loy’s true legacy was rebuilding KL after war
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Yap Ah Loy is widely remembered as the man who founded modern Kuala Lumpur, but according to his fifth-generation great-grandson Glenn Yap, that story only tells half the truth. Glenn said Yap’s defining contribution was not only founding the city, but rebuilding it after Kuala Lumpur was reduced to ruins during the Klang War, which ended in 1873, and again after a major fire and severe flooding struck the town in 1881. “People always say Yap Ah Loy founded Kuala Lumpur, but what made him extraordinary was that he rebuilt KL after it was completely destroyed during the Klang War,” he said during the Life and Legacy of Yap Ah Loy talk organised by Badan Warisan Malaysia.

items.title: Umno opens doors to ousted Bersatu leaders via Rumah Bangsa initiative, says party veep
items.summary: KLUANG, May 12 — Umno is ready to welcome former Bersatu members and leaders subjected to disciplinary action by their party to join its ranks through the Rumah Bangsa initiative, said party Vice-President Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. The Rumah Bangsa Unification Committee chairman said that as a party that serves as a home for the Malays, Umno remains open to anyone who wishes to align with the party’s struggle. Yesterday, the Bersatu Disciplinary Board sacked and suspended four more elected representatives and party leaders for violating Clause 9.1.4 of the party constitution, as well as the party’s Code of Ethics and Conduct.

items.title: Nineteen illegal factories in Pulau Indah demolished after operating for over 20 years
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 12 — A total of 19 illegal factories in Pulau Indah, Klang have been demolished including utility disconnection through the Integrated Demolition Enforcement Operation to ensure industry compliance with Selangor state land laws. Selangor Local Government and Tourism Exco Datuk Ng Sue Lim said the operation, which involved 300 personnel from various agencies, was carried out under Section 425 of the National Land Code against premises found to be operating without proper compliance. Several land expropriation applications received around 2019 to 2020 were also rejected,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: MACC: Senior executive remanded seven days over alleged RM8,000 graft in Pahang
items.summary: “Initial investigations found that the suspect is believed to have committed the offence in 2024 by assisting a construction company and a contractor company in obtaining contracts, approving work contract evaluations, securing work completion certifications, and expediting payment claims. A seven-day remand order, until May 18, against the suspect was issued by Magistrate Tengku Eliana Tuan Kamaruzaman after an application by the MACC at the Kuantan Magistrate’s Court this morning. Meanwhile, Pahang MACC director Mohd Shukor Mahmud, when contacted, confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 16(a)(A) of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Want to watch World Cup 2026 matches for free? Download ‘RTM Klik’ app, Fahmi tells Malaysians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — The public is encouraged to download the “RTM Klik” application to ensure they can watch the Fifa World Cup 2026 matches for free. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the tournament would be aired by Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) and Unifi TV as the official broadcasters of the Fifa World Cup 2026 in Malaysia. Last week, Fahmi said the Fifa World Cup 2026, to run from June 11 to July 19, would be broadcast on various platforms, including free-to-air broadcasts via MyTV as well as streaming platforms through RTM Klik and Unifi TV to ensure comprehensive access.

items.title: Police forum targets cybercrime, scams and illegal e-waste threats, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) is continuing to enhance the nation’s security preparedness through the organisation of the PDRM Special Dialogue V 2026 (PSD V 2026) in the efforts to address the increasingly complex and dynamic evolution of transborder crime threats. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the international-level dialogue platform represents a progressive step by the force to bring together technology experts, policymakers and enforcement agencies to formulate effective strategies against contemporary security threats. The programme also involved several key PDRM departments, including the Criminal Investigation Department, Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department, Commercial Crime Investigation Department, as well as the Internal Security and Public Order Department.

items.title: Defence Ministry lifts MAF procurement fund freeze after anti-leakage measures
items.summary: KLUANG, May 12 — The Defence Ministry (MINDEF) has confirmed that the freeze on procurement funds for Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) assets has been lifted following restructuring measures implemented to ensure there are no issues involving leakages or misuse of funds. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said all military asset procurement matters were now proceeding as planned. Previously, he said Mindef was discussing the status of the frozen MAF procurement funds with the Finance Ministry to ensure critical operations and preparedness were not affected.

items.title: National Economic Action Council urges local authorities to cut rental rates for traders
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir Akmal said the move is crucial to help petty traders, night market operators and micro-entrepreneurs cope with rising operating costs, which now account for an average of seven to 58 per cent of their total business expenses. Meanwhile, he said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is offering a 50 per cent rental cut for selected hawker stalls until December 2027. For the services sector, Akmal Nasrullah said the Services Producer Price Index rose 2.1 per cent in the first quarter of 2026, driven by higher prices in the accommodation, food and beverage, and transport subsectors.

items.title: Public to access global supply crisis dashboard from May 15, says Akmal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The Global Supply Crisis Monitoring Dashboard, jointly developed by the Ministry of Economy and the Department of Statistics Malaysia, is expected to be accessible to the public from May 15, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said the dashboard is intended to serve as a transparent and easily accessible central reference point for the public to monitor current developments related to the global supply crisis. “The public-facing dashboard comprises 10 main submenus, including energy, commodities, cost of living, economic performance, foreign exchange rates and trade flows,” he said during the Global Supply Crisis Briefing today.

items.title: MOH may add hantavirus tracker to MySejahtera if needed, says deputy minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) will add a hantavirus tracker to the MySejahtera application's Disease Tracker feature if the need arises, Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib said. She said the enhancement would enable users to access real-time information on the disease more easily, adding that the ministry would continue to monitor the situation. "We hope the situation remains under control and subsides.

items.title: Retired lady cop flees hit-and-run, tests positive for drugs after 50km chase from Negeri to Melaka
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — A 65-year-old former police sergeant who allegedly fled a hit-and-run crash led authorities on a dramatic 50km cross-border chase before being arrested and testing positive for amphetamines. The pursuit, which mobilised police units from both Negeri Sembilan and Melaka, eventually came to a halt when the suspect, who is retired, was intercepted along Jalan Bukit Beruang in Melaka. Acting Rembau OCPD Deputy Superintendent Azmi Ali stated that the ordeal began earlier that night in Taman Sedia Raja, Chembong, where the suspect’s car allegedly rammed into a van and a second car.

items.title: ILO urges fair hiring practices, says migrant workers must not be charged recruitment fees
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 12 — The International Labour Organisation (ILO) said migrant workers in Malaysia, particularly vulnerable groups, should not be burdened with recruitment fees and related costs when seeking employment abroad, The Star reported. In a statement, the ILO said the principle was central to fair and ethical recruitment practices, adding that its global definition of recruitment fees and related costs had become an internationally recognised benchmark since its adoption in 2018 and approval in 2019. “The definition establishes a comprehensive international standard, clarifying that recruitment fees and related costs refer to any fees or costs incurred in the recruitment process for workers to secure employment or placement, regardless of the manner, timing, or location of their imposition or collection,” it was reported.

items.title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9pc in March, says DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Malaysia’s labour market remained stable in March 2026, with the unemployment rate unchanged at 2.9 per cent, supported by continued expansion in employment and labour force participation, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). “The labour force increased by 0.1 per cent to 17.31 million persons in March 2026 from 17.30 million in February, while the labour force participation rate (LFPR) remained at 70.9 per cent,” he said in a statement today. Mohd Uzir said the number of employed persons also edged up by 0.1 per cent to 16.80 million persons compared with 16.79 million recorded in the previous month.
